title: Rich Piedmontese Cheese Cassata
description: Savory, flaky pastry filled with a zesty Pecorino and ricotta blend, baked to perfection in a tandoor for a show‑off Italian twist.
instructions: Preheat tandoor oven to 220°C and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Whisk flour, kosher salt, and black pepper in a large bowl.
Cut chilled butter into small cubes and rub into flour mixture until coarse crumbs form.
Add water gradually, stirring until dough just comes together, shape into a ball, wrap in plastic, and chill for 30 minutes.
Combine ricotta, Pecorino, smoked paprika, cumin, dried oregano, rosemary, and olive oil until evenly incorporated.
Roll chilled dough into a 30x20cm rectangle and cut into four equal strips; wrap each strip around the cheese mixture, sealing the ends.
Place the filled strips on the prepared baking sheet, brush tops with olive oil, and bake in the tandoor for 18–20 minutes until golden and flaky.
Remove from tandoor, let rest 5 minutes, then slice and serve hot.
